49.reason: The condition `num[m]!=-1` is incorrect; it should check the number of valid elements, not a sentinel value, because num array may not have -1 as a terminator, causing out-of-bounds access.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  The condition `num[m]!=-1` is incorrect; it should check the number of valid elements, not a sentinel value, because num array may not have -1 as a terminator, causing out-of-bounds access.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  The loop condition `num[m]!=-1` is incorrect because `num` array is not initialized with -1 sentinel values; this may cause out-of-bounds access or infinite loop.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The condition num[m]!=-1 never becomes false because the num array is not terminated with -1, causing an infinite loop and out-of-bounds access.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  No sentinel value -1 exists in num array; this loop may run out of bounds, causing a runtime error.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  The loop condition `num[m]!=-1` is incorrect because the `num` array is not initialized to -1, and even if it were, shifting elements doesn't guarantee a -1 terminator immediately. This leads to reading out of bounds or infinite loops. It should rely on the count of numbers or a proper termination condition linked to the operator array length.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  The loop condition `num[m]!=-1` is incorrect because the `num` array is not initialized with -1 sentinels. It contains garbage values or zeros from previous operations. This leads to accessing out-of-bounds memory or incorrect shifting logic, causing Runtime Error or logical errors. It should rely on the count of numbers or a separate length variable.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The condition `num[m]!=-1` is incorrect because the array `num` is never initialized to -1; this leads to reading uninitialized memory and undefined behavior when shifting elements]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The condition `num[m]!=-1` is incorrect because the array `num` is never initialized to -1, so this loop may run out of bounds or stop prematurely. It should instead shift elements until the end of the valid numbers (based on count), not check for -1.]

37.reason: This adds an extra number (0) at the end because aq is cleared after the last operator '=', distorting the number and operator counts.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  This adds an extra number (0) at the end because aq is cleared after the last operator '=', distorting the number and operator counts.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  This adds an extra number after the loop; the last number was already stored when encountering '=', leading to an incorrect count of numbers.

71.reason: The '=' operator remains in the op array and will be processed here; the corresponding num[t+1] may be out of bounds or produce incorrect results.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  The '=' operator remains in the op array and will be processed here; the corresponding num[t+1] may be out of bounds or produce incorrect results.

23.reason: [Using `gets()` is unsafe and may cause buffer overflow; more critically, it does not handle the '=' properly as it reads until newline, but the problem ends input with '=', so the loop parsing may include '=' as an operator]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Using `gets()` is unsafe and may cause buffer overflow; more critically, it does not handle the '=' properly as it reads until newline, but the problem ends input with '=', so the loop parsing may include '=' as an operator]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Using `gets` is unsafe and may cause buffer overflow; more critically, it reads until newline but the problem ends with '=', so it may include '=' and newline incorrectly. Also, `gets` is deprecated and removed in modern C standards.]
